desc.ur: Apna naam store karne ke liye ek variable name banao aur uski value print karo.
desc.en: Create a variable named name to store your name and print its value.

hint.ur: name = "Ahmed" likhkar variable banao, phir print(name) use karo.
hint.en: Create the variable by writing name = "Ahmed", then use print(name).

solution:
name = "Ahmed"
print(name)

desc.ur: Apna naam, umar, aur shehar store karo teen alag variables mein aur ek line mein print karo.
desc.en: Store your name, age, and city in three separate variables and print them in a single line.

example.ur: Output: Mera naam Ali hai, 20 saal, Lahore se
example.en: Output: My name is Ali, 20 years old, from Lahore

hint.ur: f-string use karo: print(f"Mera naam {name} hai, {age} saal, {city} se")
hint.en: Use an f-string: print(f"My name is {name}, {age} years old, from {city}")

solution:
name = "Ali"
age = 20
city = "Lahore"
print(f"Mera naam {name} hai, {age} saal, {city} se")

desc.ur: Integer, float aur boolean ka ek ek variable banao aur type() function se unki type print karo.
desc.en: Create one variable each for integer, float, and boolean, and print their types using the type() function.

hint.ur: type(variable_name) se kisi bhi variable ki type pata kar sakte hain.
hint.en: You can check the type of any variable using type(variable_name).

solution:
x = 10
y = 3.14
z = True
print(type(x))
print(type(y))
print(type(z))

desc.ur: User se ek number string mein milti hai (e.g., "42"). Usse integer mein convert karke uska square nikalo.
desc.en: You receive a number as a string (e.g., "42"). Convert it to an integer and output its square.

hint.ur: int() function se string ko integer mein convert karo. Square ke liye ** 2 use karo.
hint.en: Convert the string to an integer using the int() function. Use ** 2 for the square.

solution:
s = "15"
n = int(s)
print(n ** 2)

desc.ur: Do variables a=5 aur b=10 ko bina third variable ke swap karo.
desc.en: Swap two variables a=5 and b=10 without using a third variable.

example.ur:
Before: a=5, b=10
After: a=10, b=5
example.en:
Before: a=5, b=10
After: a=10, b=5

hint.ur: Python mein ek hi line mein swap hota hai: a, b = b, a
hint.en: In Python, swapping is done in a single line: a, b = b, a

solution:
a = 5
b = 10
print(f"Before: a={a}, b={b}")
a, b = b, a
print(f"After: a={a}, b={b}")

desc.ur: Ek string "  hello world  " lo aur strip() lagao, phir title case mein print karo.
desc.en: Take a string "  hello world  ", apply strip() to remove whitespace, and print it in title case.

hint.ur: .strip() spaces hatata hai, .title() har word ka pehla letter capital karta hai.
hint.en: .strip() removes spaces, .title() capitalizes the first letter of each word.

solution:
s = "  hello world  "
print(s.strip().title())

desc.ur: "Pakistan" string ki length nikalo aur har character alag line par print karo.
desc.en: Find the length of the string "Pakistan" and print each character on a separate line.

hint.ur: len() se length, for loop se characters iterate karo.
hint.en: Use len() to get length, and a for loop to iterate through the characters.

solution:
s = "Pakistan"
print(f"Length: {len(s)}")
for c in s:
    print(c)

desc.ur: Ek product ka naam aur price store karo. Formatted string print karo.
desc.en: Store a product name and its price. Print a formatted string using f-strings.

example.ur: Output: Laptop ki qeemat 85000 rupees hai.

hint.ur: f"text {variable} text" format use karo.
hint.en: Use the f"text {variable} text" format.

solution:
product = "Laptop"
price = 85000
print(f"{product} ki qeemat {price} rupees hai.")

desc.ur: "abcdefghij" string se pehle 5 characters, aakhri 3 characters, aur middle reverse nikalo.
desc.en: From the string "abcdefghij", print the first 5 characters, the last 3 characters, and the reverse of the string.

example.ur:
First 5: abcde
Last 3: hij
Reverse: jihgfedcba
example.en:
First 5: abcde
Last 3: hij
Reverse: jihgfedcba

solution:
s = "abcdefghij"
print("First 5:", s[:5])
print("Last 3:", s[-3:])
print("Reverse:", s[::-1])

desc.ur: Ek sentence mein kitne words hain? split() use karo.
desc.en: Count the number of words in a sentence. Use split().

example.ur:
Input: "Python bohat acha language hai"
Output: 5 words
example.en:
Input: "Python is a very good language"
Output: 7 words

hint.ur: .split() string ko spaces par split karta hai, len() se count karo.
hint.en: .split() splits the string at spaces, count using len().

solution:
s = "Python bohat acha language hai"
words = s.split()
print(f"{len(words)} words")

desc.ur: Check karo koi word palindrome hai ya nahi (forward aur backward same ho).
desc.en: Check if a word is a palindrome (spelled the same forward and backward).

example.ur:
Input: "madam"
Output: madam ek palindrome hai!
example.en:
Input: "madam"
Output: madam is a palindrome!

hint.ur: s == s[::-1] check karo.
hint.en: Check if s == s[::-1].

desc.ur: Ek number lo aur check karo positive hai, negative hai, ya zero.
desc.en: Take a number and check if it is positive, negative, or zero.

hint.ur: if n > 0 elif n < 0 else use karo.
hint.en: Use if n > 0, elif n < 0, else.

desc.ur: Marks check karo. 50 ya zyada = Pass, kam = Fail.
desc.en: Check marks. 50 or more = Pass, less = Fail.

hint.ur: if marks >= 50 use karo.
hint.en: Use if marks >= 50.

desc.ur: Marks ke hisaab se grade do: 90+=A, 80+=B, 70+=C, 60+=D, else F.
desc.en: Calculate grades: 90+=A, 80+=B, 70+=C, 60+=D, else F.

hint.ur: elif chain use karo har grade ke liye.
hint.en: Use an elif chain to check each grade threshold.

desc.ur: Check karo saal leap year hai ya nahi.
desc.en: Check if a year is a leap year.

example.en:
Input: 2024
Output: 2024 is a leap year

hint.ur: (year % 4 == 0 and year % 100 != 0) or (year % 400 == 0)
hint.en: Use: (year % 4 == 0 and year % 100 != 0) or (year % 400 == 0)

desc.ur: 1 se 30 tak: 3 ka multiple = Fizz, 5 ka = Buzz, dono = FizzBuzz, baqi = number.
desc.en: From 1 to 30: multiple of 3 = Fizz, multiple of 5 = Buzz, both = FizzBuzz, otherwise = number.

hint.ur: Pehle FizzBuzz check karo, phir Fizz, phir Buzz.
hint.en: Check for FizzBuzz (modulo 15) first, then Fizz (modulo 3), then Buzz (modulo 5).

desc.ur: for loop use karke 1 se 10 tak sare numbers print karo.
desc.en: Print all numbers from 1 to 10 using a for loop.

hint.ur: for i in range(1, 11): print(i)
hint.en: Use for i in range(1, 11): print(i)

solution:
for i in range(1, 11):
    print(i)

desc.ur: 5 ka pahara (multiplication table) print karo.
desc.en: Print the multiplication table of 5.

hint.ur: for i in range(1, 11): print(f"5 x {i} = {5*i}")
hint.en: Use for i in range(1, 11): print(f"5 x {i} = {5*i}")

solution:
for i in range(1, 11):
    print(f"5 x {i} = {5*i}")

desc.ur: 1 se 100 tak sare numbers ka sum nikalo.
desc.en: Find the sum of all numbers from 1 to 100.

hint.ur: total = 0, phir har number add karo. Ya sum(range(1,101)) use karo.
hint.en: Set total = 0, then add each number. Or use sum(range(1,101)).

solution:
total = 0
for i in range(1, 101):
    total += i
print(f"Sum: {total}")
# Ya ek line mein:
# print(sum(range(1, 101)))

desc.ur: Ek number ka factorial nikalo.
desc.en: Calculate the factorial of a number.

hint.ur: result = 1, phir 1 se n tak multiply karo.
hint.en: Start with result = 1, then multiply from 1 to n.

solution:
n = 5
result = 1
for i in range(1, n+1):
    result *= i
print(f"{n}! = {result}")

desc.ur: 1 se 50 ke beech sare prime numbers print karo.
desc.en: Print all prime numbers between 1 and 50.

hint.ur: Nested loop: har number ke liye check karo koi factor hai ya nahi.
hint.en: Nested loop: check if there is any factor for each number in the range.

desc.ur: 5 fruits ki list banao, append se 1 aur add karo, phir sort karke print karo.
desc.en: Create a list of 5 fruits, append one more, sort it, and print.

hint.ur: fruits = ["apple",...], fruits.append("mango"), fruits.sort()
hint.en: fruits = ["apple",...], fruits.append("mango"), fruits.sort()

solution:
fruits = ["cherry", "apple", "banana", "grape", "orange"]
fruits.append("mango")
fruits.sort()
print(fruits)

desc.ur: Ek list mein pehla, aakhri aur beech wala element access karo.
desc.en: Access the first, last, and middle element in a list.

hint.ur: list[0], list[-1], list[len(list)//2]
hint.en: Use list[0], list[-1], and list[len(list)//2]

solution:
nums = [10, 20, 30, 40, 50]
print("First:", nums[0])
print("Last:", nums[-1])
print("Middle:", nums[len(nums)//2])

desc.ur: 1 se 20 tak sare even numbers ki list ek line mein banao.
desc.en: Create a list of all even numbers from 1 to 20 in a single line using list comprehension.

hint.ur: [x for x in range(2, 21, 2)] ya [x for x in range(1,21) if x%2==0]
hint.en: Use: [x for x in range(2, 21, 2)] or [x for x in range(1,21) if x%2==0]

solution:
evens = [x for x in range(1, 21) if x % 2 == 0]
print(evens)

desc.ur: Bina max()/min() use kiye list mein sab se bada aur chota number nikalo.
desc.en: Find the largest and smallest number in a list without using max() or min().

hint.ur: Pehla element assume karo max/min, phir loop mein compare karo.
hint.en: Assume the first element is the max and min, then compare each element in a loop.

desc.ur: List se duplicate elements hatao without using set().
desc.en: Remove duplicate elements from a list without using set().

hint.ur: Nayi list banao, sirf woh elements add karo jo abhi tak nahin hain.
hint.en: Create a new list, only append elements that are not already present.

desc.ur: Ek student ka dictionary banao name, age, marks ke sath aur print karo.
desc.en: Create a student dictionary with keys name, age, marks and print it.

hint.ur: student = {"name":"Ali", "age":20, "marks":85}
hint.en: student = {"name":"Ali", "age":20, "marks":85}

solution:
student = {"name": "Ali", "age": 20, "marks": 85}
print(f"Name: {student['name']}, Age: {student['age']}, Marks: {student['marks']}")

desc.ur: Ek sentence mein har word kitni baar aaya gino.
desc.en: Count the frequency of each word in a sentence.

example.ur:
Input: "the cat sat on the mat"
Output: {'the': 2, 'cat': 1, ...}
example.en:
Input: "the cat sat on the mat"
Output: {'the': 2, 'cat': 1, ...}

hint.ur: Dict mein word key ho, count value. dict.get(word, 0) + 1 use karo.
hint.en: Let word be key, count be value. Use dict.get(word, 0) + 1.

desc.ur: Phone book program: names aur numbers store karo, search karo, add karo.
desc.en: Phone book program: store names and numbers, search, and add contacts.

hint.ur: Dict use karo, input() se search karo, .get() se safely access karo.
hint.en: Use a dict, search with keys, safely access using .get().

solution:
contacts = {"Ali": "0300-1234567", "Sara": "0321-9876543"}
new_name = "Bilal"
new_num = "0311-5555555"
contacts[new_name] = new_num
search = "Ali"
result = contacts.get(search, "Contact nahi mila")
print(f"{search}: {result}")
print(f"Total contacts: {len(contacts)}")

desc.ur: Ek function banao jo kisi ko greet kare.
desc.en: Create a function that greets a user by name.

hint.ur: def greet(name): ... return f"Salam {name}!"
hint.en: Use: def greet(name): ... return f"Salam {name}!"

solution:
def greet(name):
    return f"Salam {name}! Kya haal hai?"

print(greet("Ahmed"))
print(greet("Sara"))

desc.ur: Ek function banao jo do numbers leke +, -, *, / sab return kare.
desc.en: Create a function that takes two numbers and returns sum, diff, product, quotient.

hint.ur: def calc(a, b): return a+b, a-b, a*b, a/b
hint.en: Use: def calc(a, b): return a+b, a-b, a*b, a/b

desc.ur: Ek function banao coffee order karne ke liye. Default size "medium" ho.
desc.en: Create a function to order coffee. Let the default size be "medium".

hint.ur: def order(coffee, size="medium"): ...
hint.en: Use: def order(coffee, size="medium"): ...

solution:
def order(coffee, size="medium"):
    return f"{size.title()} {coffee} order hua"

print(order("Cappuccino"))
print(order("Latte", "large"))

desc.ur: Recursive function se factorial nikalo.
desc.en: Calculate the factorial of a number using recursion.

hint.ur: Base case: if n <= 1: return 1. Recursive: return n * factorial(n-1)
hint.en: Base case: if n <= 1: return 1. Recursive: return n * factorial(n-1)

desc.ur: Flexible function banao jo kisi bhi number of arguments accept kare.
desc.en: Create flexible functions that accept any number of positional (*args) and keyword (**kwargs) arguments.

example.ur:
add(1,2,3,4,5) -> 15
info(name="Ali", age=20) -> Name: Ali, Age: 20
example.en:
add(1,2,3,4,5) -> 15
info(name="Ali", age=20) -> Name: Ali, Age: 20

hint.ur: *args tuple banata hai, **kwargs dictionary.
hint.en: *args forms a tuple, **kwargs forms a dictionary.

desc.ur: Ek simple Car class banao make, model, year ke sath. describe() method ho.
desc.en: Create a simple Car class with make, model, year and a describe() method.

hint.ur: class Car: def __init__(self, make, model, year): ...
hint.en: class Car: def __init__(self, make, model, year): ...

desc.ur: BankAccount class: deposit(), withdraw(), get_balance(). Negative withdraw allow na karo.
desc.en: BankAccount class: implement deposit(), withdraw(), get_balance() with balance validation.

hint.ur: __init__ mein balance = 0. withdraw mein check karo balance sufficient hai.
hint.en: Set balance = 0 in constructor. In withdraw, check if balance is sufficient.

desc.ur: Animal class banao. Dog aur Cat inherit karein aur apna speak() override karein.
desc.en: Create Animal, Dog, and Cat classes. Override speak() in subclasses.

hint.ur: class Dog(Animal): def speak(self): return "Bhow!"
hint.en: class Dog(Animal): def speak(self): return "Woof!"

desc.ur: Kuch lines ek file mein likho, phir wapas parhke print karo.
desc.en: Write a few lines to a file, then read and print the contents.

example.ur: File banao "test.txt", 3 lines likho, phir parho.
example.en: Create file "test.txt", write 3 lines, then read them.

hint.ur: with open("test.txt", "w") as f: f.write(...)
hint.en: Use: with open("test.txt", "w") as f: f.write(...)

solution:
# File mein likhna
with open("test.txt", "w") as f:
    f.write("Pehli line\n")
    f.write("Doosri line\n")
    f.write("Teesri line\n")

# File se parhna
with open("test.txt", "r") as f:
    content = f.read()
print(content)

desc.ur: Sorted list mein binary search implement karo.
desc.en: Implement binary search in a sorted list.

hint.ur: mid = (low+high)//2. Target se compare karo.
hint.en: Set mid = (low+high)//2. Compare with target value.

desc.ur: Bubble sort implement karo without sorted() use kiye.
desc.en: Implement bubble sort without using the built-in sorted() function.

hint.ur: Nested loop: adjacent elements compare karo, swap karo agar zarurat ho.
hint.en: Nested loop: compare adjacent elements, swap them if they are in the wrong order.
